rows.notes: Consensus on row type and no amounts. 'Batts' from gemini-flash matches image slightly better than 'Baths' from gpt-5-mini.

rows.description: Reed of Mr Hatching for increments for the half year ending at Bapt. 1706
rows.notes: Chosen from gemini-flash. Description is more accurate. Visual verification confirms amounts: '38' in pounds, a clear blank space in shillings (interpreted as 0s), and '7' in pence column. gpt-5-mini incorrectly read 7 shillings and 0 pence.
rows.english_description: Receipt from Mr Hatching for increments (college charges) for the half year ending at Michaelmas/Baptism 1706.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from Mr Hatching for increments for the half year ending at Bapt. 1706, amounting to £38 0s 7d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt on account of the new buildings, amounting to £23 1s 0d.

rows.notes: Consensus on row type and no amounts. Description from gemini-flash ('Comoners', 'reed') is visually more accurate than gpt-5-mini ('Commoners', 'recd').

rows.description: Mr Fra: Buncombe 8s Mr Rich ye 0 8s
rows.notes: Visual verification: This is a description of an entry. The '8s' is part of the description text, not a formal amount in the right-hand columns. Both extractors provided incorrect amounts or interpretation for this line. Description from gemini-flash is accurate.
rows.english_description: Receipts from gentlemen commoners: Mr Francis Buncombe and Mr Richard, each contributing eight shillings.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipts from gentlemen commoners Francis Buncombe and Richard, each paying 8s.

rows.description: Mr Rich Duck 8s Mr Will: Clobery 8s
rows.notes: Visual verification: Similar to the previous row, this is a description line for an entry, but no formal amounts are present in the right-hand columns. The '8s' is part of the description text. The '16 00' visible next to this line is a subtotal for the preceding *group* of entries. Both extractors provided incorrect amounts or interpretation. Description from gemini-flash is accurate.
rows.english_description: Receipts from gentlemen commoners Richard Duck and William Clobery, each contributing eight shillings.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipts from Richard Duck and William Clobery, each paying 8s.

rows.notes: Missing row added. This '16 00' is visually a subtotal for the preceding bracketed entries (Row 5 & 6). It is located below the brace for the group but functions as a sum. No explicit label for this subtotal in the ledger, hence a descriptive note added. Neither extractor correctly identified this as a distinct group total.

Visual verification confirms this is a header, introducing the list of names below. No amounts are present on this specific line. gpt-5-mini incorrectly identified amounts for this line (from the previous section's total). Description from gemini-flash is more accurate.

rows.description: Mr Williams 2s Mr Haviland 2s Mr Blake 2s

Both extractors provided incorrect amounts or interpretation. Description from gemini-flash is accurate. gpt-5-mini split these into individual names, which is not how the text is physically laid out.

Description adapted for clarity as there is no specific label beyond the brace.

Consensus on major amounts. Visual verification confirms a clear 'q' symbol for 0.25 farthing in the pence column, as noted by gemini-flash. gpt-5-mini missed this fraction, stating the pence column was empty. Description from gemini-flash is also more accurate.
Full consensus between extractors on description, row type, and no amounts.

rows.notes: Consensus on description and pounds/shillings. Visual verification confirms pence as '1' followed by a clear 'q' symbol, indicating 0.25 farthing, as noted by gemini-flash. gpt-5-mini's pence reading of '6' is incorrect and it missed the fraction.

rows.description: - Memdum. the whole was 2s. but 1s. 6d. ob was sett off.
rows.notes: Chosen from gemini-flash. Visual verification confirms this is a distinct descriptive line with no amounts in the formal columns. gpt-5-mini incorrectly merged this line with the following one, and had transcription errors in the combined description. gemini-flash's note identifies it as a descriptive note, which is accurate.
rows.english_description: Memorandum that the total was 2s but 1s 6d was set off (deducted).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Memorandum noting the total was 2s, with 1s 6d set off.

rows.description: For arrears of balles in the year 1704.
rows.notes: Chosen from gemini-flash. Visual verification confirms this is a distinct entry line. gpt-5-mini incorrectly merged this with the preceding memorandum. gemini-flash's transcription of description and amounts (£0 13s 6d) is accurate, with good notes explaining the slight ambiguity of 'balles' and the pence '6' flourish.
rows.english_description: Receipt for arrears of 'balles' (dues) relating to the year 1704.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt for arrears of balles for the year 1704, amounting to £0 13s 6d.

rows.notes: Visual verification clearly shows £20 9s 7 3q, matching gemini-flash's amounts and fraction. gpt-5-mini misread pounds as 10 and the fraction as 0.5. No explicit description for this subtotal in the ledger itself, hence an empty description.

Consensus on major amounts. Visual verification confirms the 'ob' symbol for 0.5 halfpenny in the pence column, as noted by gemini-flash. gpt-5-mini missed the fraction, citing smudge/shadow. Description from gemini-flash is also more accurate.
